name: Taxonomy Mapping review for Dna Repair Enhancement bundle
summary: The taxonomy mapping is appropriate for this revision. DREAM remains mapped to transposon-and-somatic-mutation-control and is additionally mapped to dna-repair-enhancement because the source describes DREAM as a DNA repair repressor tied to mutation burden; the staged outlook also keeps NAD, nucleotide, telomere, oncology, and disease-boundary sources outside the support map unless they have direct repair endpoints or repair-regulator claims.

findings.claim_or_issue: The staged study/finding adds dna-repair-enhancement while retaining transposon-and-somatic-mutation-control.
findings.why_it_matters: The source fits both a somatic-mutation-control lens and a DNA repair regulation lens; dropping either mapping would make discovery and support-map reuse less accurate.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the dual track mapping and the DNA repair enhancement tag.

findings.claim_or_issue: NAD precursor, nucleotide, telomere, oncology DDR, and DNA repair syndrome boundary records are mentioned as gaps or future coverage-repair scope rather than added as current support for DNA Repair Enhancement.
findings.why_it_matters: This prevents adjacent biology from inflating the DNA Repair Enhancement evidence base without direct repair endpoints or repair-regulator evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; keep the boundary language and defer source-completeness work to the recommended coverage-repair pass.
